name: GPT-4 Minority-Class Science Response Augmentation
population_or_context: Student-written short open-ended responses to four science items, including binary and four-category scoring tasks.
sample_size: Original datasets of 266 or 374 responses per task; GPT-4 generated 120 or 160 minority-class augmentations per task in the reported setup.
methods: Structured GPT-4 prompts generated minority-class science responses; DistilBERT scorers were fine-tuned on original and augmented datasets and compared with models trained with additional authentic student responses.
education_context: Middle-school science assessment items asking students to write one to five sentence explanations of science phenomena.
response_origin: Hybrid: real student responses plus GPT-4-generated responses for underrepresented scoring classes.
rubric_label_space: Binary labels for two tasks and four-category holistic labels based on NGSS-oriented DCI and SEP/CCC criteria for two tasks.
label_source: Original responses were scored by subject matter experts with reported interrater reliability; generated responses inherited the target minority-class condition from the prompt and were compared against additional authentic minority-class responses.
generation_method: Prompt-based generation of new responses conditioned on item statement, scoring rubric, examples, grouping rules, and a minority-class response to augment.
prompt_strategy: Structured WRVRT-reviewed prompts; role prompting as a data augmenting machine; temperature 0 and top_p 0.01 to preserve scientific semantics.
evaluation_method: Fine-tuned DistilBERT scoring models were evaluated with accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 under varying proportions of augmented data and against a gold-standard additional-student-response condition.
scorer_use: Training augmentation and class balancing for automatic scoring model development.
real_response_comparator: Yes. GPT-4 augmentation was compared with additional authentic student-written minority-class responses matched in sample size.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Student responses were imbalanced across scoring categories, with insufficient minority-class cases for scorer training.
synthetic_data_research_role: Generated minority-class responses supplemented sparse score categories to train and benchmark automatic scorers.
reported_metrics: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1; stable improvement generally required about 20% to 40% augmented data in the abstracted version.
limitations: Effects varied by task; the authors note that augmented data may not fully capture the complexity and variability of true minority-class responses.
